# 'downstack merge' handles server-side squash merges.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-05-24T19:33:15Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub config mergeMethod squash
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create a stack: main -> feature1 -> feature2 -> feature3
cp $WORK/extra/stack-feature1.txt stack.txt
git add stack.txt
gs bc feature1 -m 'Add feature 1'
cp $WORK/extra/stack-feature2.txt stack.txt
git add stack.txt
gs bc feature2 -m 'Add feature 2'
cp $WORK/extra/stack-feature3.txt stack.txt
git add stack.txt
gs bc feature3 -m 'Add feature 3'

# submit the stack
gs downstack submit --fill
stderr 'Created #1'
stderr 'Created #2'
stderr 'Created #3'

# merge feature1 and feature2 from feature2.
# ShamHub will squash the merge because of the server-side default above.
gs bco feature2
env ROBOT_INPUT=$WORK/robot-merge.golden ROBOT_OUTPUT=$WORK/robot-merge.actual
gs downstack merge
cmp $WORK/robot-merge.actual $WORK/robot-merge.golden
stderr 'Merging feature1'
stderr 'feature1: #1 was merged'
stderr 'feature1: deleted'
stderr 'Merging feature2'
stderr 'feature3: retargeted upstack onto main'
stderr 'feature2: #2 was merged'
stderr 'feature2: deleted'

# feature3 still has the full file content before replay.
gs bco feature3
cmp stack.txt $WORK/extra/stack-feature3.txt

# Explicit restack should replay only the surviving upstack change
# on top of the squashed trunk commit.
gs branch restack
stderr 'feature3: restacked on main'
cmp stack.txt $WORK/extra/stack-feature3.txt
git log --format=%s main..feature3
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature3-log.txt

-- extra/stack-feature1.txt --
feature 1
-- extra/stack-feature2.txt --
feature 1
feature 2
-- extra/stack-feature3.txt --
feature 1
feature 2
feature 3
-- repo/.keep --
-- golden/feature3-log.txt --
Add feature 3
-- robot-merge.golden --
===
> Merge 2 change(s) bottom-up?: [Y/n]
>   feature1 (#1)
>   feature2 (#2)
true
